rows.description: Computus Math: Hole. Rectoriq a Secunda Die Novembris 1724
rows.notes: Top title line. Handwriting partly faded and some letters abbreviated (e.g. 'Rectoriq'). Capital words visible but several letters uncertain (e.g. 'Math/Mat.' and 'Hole').
rows.section_header: Computus Math: Hole. Rectoriq a Secunda Die Novembris 1724

rows.notes: Date phrase readable though the script is slightly compressed; 'Decembris 1725' is clear, 'ad eundem diem' is legible albeit in old-style script.
Section heading (looks like 'Arrearage' / 'Arrearige'). Clear as a structural label with no amounts in the row itself.
Pounds/shillings/pence columns are well aligned for this first monetary row. '60 0 0' is clear and passes currency validation.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Vincent, amounting to £60 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds '58' and shillings '3' are legible. The rightmost pence area shows a small mark that could be '2' with an extraneous flourish; original looks like '29' but 29 pence invalid, so interpreted as 2d based on alignment with pence column. Significant ambiguity remains.
rows.english_description: Annual rent due from William Long.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from William Long, amounting to £58 3s 2d.

rows.notes: The entry's pounds '29' and zero shillings/pence are clearly written and aligned in the columns. The name is slightly uncertain but appears to be a regular '... pro anno' entry.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Machellin, amounting to £29 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount '50 0 0' is clearly written and column alignment is unambiguous. The place/name text is slightly abbreviated in the manuscript ('South ...'), but this is clearly an entry with full pounds only.
rows.english_description: Annual rent due from South Warrington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from South Warrington, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds '6', shillings '7' and pence '5' read from the three narrow right columns. There is a small trailing flourish near the pence that slightly reduces confidence, but all digits fall within valid ranges.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quinquennial rent due from Jardington, amounting to £6 7s 5d.

rows.description: Horton pr quadriennio (note: 'cant. divid: quare whether this part ...')
rows.notes: Shillings '18' and pence '7' are aligned in the s and d columns; the pounds column shows a small 0. The descriptive text contains an extended marginal note and is partially abbreviated and cramped, reducing text confidence.
rows.english_description: Quadrennial rent due from Horton; entry includes a note about a division.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quadriennial rent due from Horton, amounting to £0 18s 7d.

rows.notes: The rightmost three columns show '11 13 1' aligned opposite this entry. Ink is slightly lighter but digits are isolated and within normal ranges.
rows.english_description: Annual rent due from Chace Hill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Chace Hill, amounting to £11 13s 1d.

rows.notes: Reads as '4 3 5' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Handwriting of the place name is moderately ambiguous, but the numbers are aligned and valid.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quadrennial rent due from Thorp, amounting to £4 3s 5d.

rows.notes: This line shows a clear '10 0 0' in the columns. The surname 'Barret' is legible and the 'pr biennio' abbreviation is consistent with other rows.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial rent due from Barret, amounting to £10 0s 0d.

rows.notes: The description is faint and uncertain (reads 'Calest' or 'Celest'), and the amount in the pounds column is small and faint (looks like '1 0 0'). Due to faded ink and uncertainty about the name, confidence is low.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quadrennial rent due from Calest, amounting to £1 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Name and alias phrasing visible though cramped. Amount columns read '4 15 7' clearly aligned; the script for the alias reduces textual confidence slightly.
rows.english_description: Annual rent due from Biggs (alias Lord).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Biggs alias Lord, amounting to £4 15s 7d.

rows.description: Cudlington Mill pr quinquenio paid in part
rows.notes: Description contains a visible note 'paid in part' which is legible. The numeric columns read roughly '2 10 2' though ink blur and a small blot near the pence reduce confidence.
rows.english_description: Quinquennial rent due from Cudlington Mill; noted as paid in part.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quinquennial rent due from Cudlington Mill, part payment recorded amounting to £2 10s 2d.

rows.notes: Numeric trio aligned as '4 15 8' opposite this entry. The place name 'Bampton' is slightly faded but readable in context of surrounding list.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quadrennial rent due from Bampton, amounting to £4 15s 8d.

rows.notes: '7 1 11' appears in the columns but the pence area has a dark flourish that could be a decoration; chosen reading '11d' since it fits the column and is legible on close inspection.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quadrennial rent due from Montacute, amounting to £7 1s 11d.

rows.notes: The pounds column reads '6' and both s/d columns are '0 0'. The name 'Clifton Dorry' is written in a flowing script that is moderately legible.
rows.english_description: Annual rent due from Clifton Dorry.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Clifton Dorry, amounting to £6 0s 0d.

rows.notes: This line clearly records a larger sum '26 10 0' in the three columns. The place name is a little ambiguous but clearly a list-item; numeric alignment is strong.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial rent due from Bemprington, amounting to £26 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Columns read '13 6 8' clearly. The place/name is written in a contracted hand; the numbers are clean and within valid ranges.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Prothewse, amounting to £13 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Shows '8 0 0' in the numeric columns. The heading 'Lallane pr Sequinanno' is a best-effort reading of an abbreviated Latin phrase; text confidence moderate.
rows.english_description: Rent due from Lallane for a sesquiannual period (one and a half years).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent due from Lallane for a sesquiannual period, amounting to £8 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Small sum '1 3 4' visible; the name is faint and could be 'Nidderham' or similar. Numeric columns are legible but description uncertain.
rows.english_description: Biennial rent due from Fox of Niddenham.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial rent due from Fox of Niddenham, amounting to £1 3s 4d.

rows.notes: Right columns read '0 10 0' (10 shillings). 'Backgate' looks plausible in the left text; ink is slightly lighter but columns align well.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial rent due from Backgate, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.notes: The manuscript on this row is partly obscured by a diagonal shadow; however, the numeric reading '20 0 0' is visible at the right edge. The name 'Hugh' is not certain due to the shadow.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Hugh, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.notes: The descriptive text is readable only with difficulty (shadow and ink fade). No clear numeric figures directly aligned to this row are legible; likely grouped with adjacent lines. Left amounts may be part of previous/next row.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Mysen; amount not recorded in the structured fields.

rows.notes: Near-bottom entry with clear '20 0 0' in the right columns. The place name reads as 'Lardon Hall' or similar; numeric columns are distinct and valid.
rows.english_description: Annual rent due from Lardon Hall.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from Lardon Hall, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Final descriptive line before the total is partly crossed/ink-blotted and the numeric entry (if any) is not clearly aligned. The phrase 'pr biennio' and 'part' are legible; no safe numeric reading possible.
rows.english_description: Biennial rent due from the remainder of Cudlington; noted as a part payment.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial rent due from Cudlington Rest, part payment recorded; amount not given in the structured fields.

rows.notes: Bottom-right total clearly written as '325. 19. 02.' This row provides a strong alignment reference for the pounds/shillings/pence column positions on the page.
